modules.title: Overview and Introduction
topics: Overview of software development methodology and software quality model, different models of software development and their issues.

Introduction to software architecture, evolution of software architecture, software components and connectors, common software architecture frameworks, architecture business cycle – architectural patterns – reference model.

modules.title: Architecture Models and Styles
topics: Software architecture models: structural models, framework models, dynamic models, process models.

Architectural styles: dataflow architecture, pipes and filters architecture, call‑and‑return architecture, data‑centered architecture, layered architecture, agent‑based architecture, micro‑services architecture, reactive architecture, representational state transfer (REST) architecture, etc.

modules.title: Implementation Technologies
topics: Software Architecture Description Languages (ADLs), Struts, Hibernate, Node.js, AngularJS, J2EE – JSP, Servlets, EJBs; middleware: JDBC, JNDI, JMS, RMI and CORBA, etc.
Role of UML in software architecture.

modules.title: Analysis, Design, and Evaluation
topics: Requirements for architecture and the life‑cycle view of architecture design and analysis methods.
Architecture‑based economic analysis: Cost Benefit Analysis Method (CBAM), Architecture Tradeoff Analysis Method (ATAM).
Active Reviews for Intermediate Design (ARID), Attribute‑Driven Design (ADD), architecture reuse, domain‑specific software architecture.

modules.title: Documentation
topics: Principles of sound documentation, refinement, context diagrams, variability, software interfaces.
Documenting the behavior of software elements and software systems, documentation package using a seven‑part template.

reference_books: Bennett, Douglas, “Designing Hard Software: The Essential Tasks”, Prentice-Hall, 1997.
Clements, Paul, R. Kazman, M. Klein, “Evaluating Software Architectures: Methods and Case Studies”, Addison Wesley, 2001.
Albin, S. “The Art of Software Architecture _”_, Indiana: Wiley, 2003.
Robert Mee, and Randy Stafford, “Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture”, Addison-Wesley, 2002.
Witt, B., T. Baker and E. Meritt, “Software Architecture and Design: Principles, Models and Methods”, Nostrand Reinhold, 1994.

modules.title: Fuzzy Systems
topics: Fuzzy set theory – fuzzy sets and operations
Fuzzy logic – fuzzy rules and inferencing
Fuzzy control – selection of membership functions

modules.title: Rough Set Theory and Related Models
topics: Attributes and optimization in rough sets

reference_books: Russell C. Eberhart and Yuhui Shi, Computational Intelligence: Concepts to Implementations, Morgan Kaufmann Publishers.
Andries P. Engelbrecht, Computational Intelligence: An Introduction, Wiley Publishing.
Simon Haykin, Neural Networks: A Comprehensive Foundation, Prentice Hall.
David E. Goldberg, Genetic Algorithm in Search Optimization and Machine Learning, Pearson Education.
Jagdish Chand Bansal, Pramod Kumar Singh, Nikhil R. Pal, Evolutionary and Swarm Intelligence Algorithms, Springer Publishing, 2019.
S. Rajeskaran, G.A. VijaylakshmiPai, “Neural Networks, Fuzzy Logic, Genetic Algorithms Synthesis and Applications”.
J.S. Roger Jang, C.T. Sun, E. Mizutani, “Neuro-Fuzzy and Soft Computing: A Computational Approach to Learning & Machine Intelligence”, PHI, 2002.

modules.title: Autoencoders and Regularization
topics: Autoencoders and their relation to PCA

modules.title: Convolutional Architectures and Pre‑training
topics: Recent trends in deep learning architectures

modules.title: Fundamentals of Reinforcement Learning
topics: Bandit algorithms – UCB, PAC, Median Elimination
Dynamic programming – value iteration, policy iteration

modules.title: Advanced RL Techniques
topics: Learning policies by imitating optimal controllers
Policy Gradient algorithms for full RL

reference_books: Goodfellow, I., Bengio, Y., Courville, A. – Deep Learning (MIT Press)
Duda, R. O., Hart, P. E., Stork, D. G. – Pattern Classification (Wiley)
Sutton, R. S., Barto, A. G. – Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction (2nd Edition)
Wiering, M., van Otterlo, M. (Eds.) – Reinforcement Learning: State‑of‑the‑Art

modules.title: Review of Traditional Networks
topics: Traditional networks: LAN, MAN, WAN, Intranet, Internet
TCP/IP protocol architecture: ARP/RARP, IP addressing, IP datagram format, routing table format, ICMP messages, subnetting, supernetting, CIDR, DNS
Network Address Translation (NAT): private addressing, SNAT, DNAT, NAT firewalls
VLANs: concepts, comparison with LANs, VLAN types, tagging
IPv6: address structure, address space, header format

modules.title: Traditional Routing and Transport
topics: Routing protocols – BGP: hidden networks, autonomous system concept, BGP messages
TCP segment format, sockets, synchronization, three‑way handshake, variable window size, flow control, timeout and retransmission algorithms, connection control, silly window syndrome

modules.title: Wireless LAN, Mobile IP and Ad Hoc Routing
topics: Wireless LAN transmission media and MAC problems (hidden/exposed terminals, near/far terminals)
IEEE 802.11 system architecture, protocol architecture, physical layer, spread‑spectrum concepts, MAC management, power management, security mechanisms
Mobile IP: limitations of traditional IP, goals, terminology, agent advertisement/discovery, registration, tunneling techniques
Ad‑hoc network routing vs traditional IP routing, routing protocol categories, examples OLSR, DSDV, DSR, ZRP

modules.title: Mobile Transport Layer and Cellular Networks
topics: Limitations of traditional TCP for mobile environments
Mobile‑specific transport protocols: I‑TCP, S‑TCP, M‑TCP
Cellular network fundamentals and comparison with WLANs
GSM services, system architecture, localization, call handling, handover, roaming

modules.title: Mobile Operating Systems, SDKs and M‑Commerce
topics: Mobile device operating systems: special constraints, requirements, commercial OS examples
M‑Commerce structure, advantages and disadvantages, mobile payment systems, security concerns

reference_books: W. Richard Stevens, “TCP/IP Illustrated Vol‑I”, Addison‑Wesley.
C.K. Toh, “AdHoc Mobile Wireless Networks”, First Edition, Pearson Education.
Uwe Hansmann, Lothar Merk, Martin S. Nicklons and Thomas Stober, “Principles of Mobile Computing”, Springer.
Android Developers : http://developer.android.com/index.html
Apple Developer : https://developer.apple.com/
Windows Phone Dev Center : http://developer.windowsphone.com/
BlackBerry Developer : http://developer.blackberry.com/.

modules.title: Introduction to Big Data
topics: Desired properties of Big Data system

modules.title: Hadoop Ecosystem and Processing
topics: Managing Resources and Application with Hadoop YARN

modules.title: Hive and Pig for Data Processing
topics: Operators, functions, and data types of Pig

modules.title: NoSQL Databases
topics: Variations of NoSQL architectural patterns using NoSQL to Manage Big Data

modules.title: Social Network Graph Mining
topics: Direct Discovery of communities in a social graph

Michael Minelli, Michele Chambers, AmbigaDhiraj, “Big Data Big Analytics”, Wiley

modules.title: Advanced Encryption Standard and Public‑Key Cryptosystem
Message Authentication, Digital Signatures, Key Management and Hash Functions
Information Security and Network Security
topics: Firewalls – Design and Types of Firewalls
Email Security – Services and attacks through emails
Web Security – SSL/TLS and basic security protocols

modules.title: Cryptography and Information Security Tools
topics: Vulnerability scanning tools (Angry IP, HPing2, IP Scanner, Global Network Inventory Scanner, Net Tools Suite Pack)
Steganography tools (STools, Steghide, Steganos, Stegdetect, StegSpy)
Trojan detection tools (Netstat, fPort, TCPView, CurrPorts, Process Viewer)
DoS attack understanding tools (Jolt2, Bubonic.c, Land and LaTierra, Targa, Nemesy Blast, Panther2, Crazy Pinger, Some Trouble, UDP Flood, FSMax)

reference_books: Cryptography and Network Security Principles and Practice, Fourth Edition, William Stallings, Pearson Education.
Network Security Essentials: Applications and Standards, by William Stallings, Prentice Hall.
Behrouz A. Ferouzan, “Cryptography and Network Security”, Tata McGraw‑Hill, 2007.
Charles P. Pfleeger, Shari Lawrence Pfleeger, “Security in Computing”, 4th Edition, Prentice Hall of India, 2006.
Introduction to Modern Cryptography by Jonathan Katz and Yehuda Lindell, Chapman and Hall/CRC.

modules.title: Introduction to Data & Data Mining
topics: Data Mining vs Knowledge Discovery in Databases

reference_books: Margaret H. Dunham, “Data Mining : Introductory and Advanced topics”, Pearson Edu., 2009.
Anahory & Murray, “Data Warehousing in the Real World”, Pearson Edu., 2009.

modules.title: Fundamentals of Agile Process
topics: Overview of Agile Development Models: Scrum, Extreme Programming, Feature Driven Development, Crystal, Kanban, Lean Software Development

modules.title: Agile Projects
topics: Planning for Agile Teams: Scrum Teams, XP Teams, General Agile Teams, Team Distribution
Agile Project Lifecycles: Typical Agile Project Lifecycles, Phase Activities, Product Vision
Release Planning: Creating the Product Backlog, User Stories, Prioritizing and Estimating, Creating the Release Plan
Monitoring and Adapting: Managing Risks and Issues, Retrospectives

modules.title: Introduction to Scrum
topics: Sprinting: Planning, Execution, Review and Retrospective

modules.title: Introduction to Extreme Programming (XP)
topics: XP Concepts: Refactoring, Technical Debt, Timeboxing, Stories, Velocity
Applying XP: Pair Programming, Collaborating, Release, Planning, Development

modules.title: Agile Software Design and Development
topics: Agility and Quality Assurance: Agile Interaction Design

reference_books: Robert C. Martin, Agile Software Development- Principles, Patterns and Practices, Prentice Hall, 2013.
Kenneth S. Rubin, Essential Scrum: A Practical Guide to the Most Popular Agile Process, Addison Wesley, 2012.
James Shore and Shane Warden, The Art of Agile Development, O’Reilly Media, 2007.
Craig Larman, Agile and Iterative Development: A manager’s Guide, Addison-Wesley, 2004.

modules.title: INTRODUCTION TO DISASTERS
topics: Definition of Disaster, Hazard, Vulnerability, Resilience, Risks
Types of disasters: Earthquake, Landslide, Flood, Drought, Fire, etc.
Classification, Causes, and Impacts (social, economic, political, environmental, health, psychosocial)
Differential impacts based on caste, class, gender, age, location, disability
Global trends in disasters: urban disasters, pandemics, complex emergencies, climate change
Dos and Don'ts during various types of disasters

modules.title: APPROACHES TO DISASTER RISK REDUCTION
topics: Culture of safety, prevention, mitigation, and preparedness
Roles and responsibilities of community, PRIs/ULBs, States, Centre, and other stakeholders
Institutional processes and frameworks at state and central levels
Early warning systems and advisories from appropriate agencies

modules.title: INTER‑RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN DISASTERS AND DEVELOPMENT
topics: Factors affecting vulnerabilities and differential impacts
Impact of development projects (dams, embankments, land‑use changes)
Climate change adaptation and IPCC scenarios in the Indian context
Relevance of indigenous knowledge, appropriate technology, and local resources

modules.title: DISASTER RISK MANAGEMENT IN INDIA
topics: Hazard and vulnerability profile of India
Components of disaster relief: water, food, sanitation, shelter, health, waste management
Institutional arrangements for mitigation, response, and preparedness
Disaster Management Act, related policies, plans, programmes, and legislation
Role of GIS and Information Technology in preparedness, risk assessment, response, and recovery

modules.title: DISASTER MANAGEMENT: APPLICATIONS AND CASE STUDIES AND FIELD WORKS
topics: Earthquake vulnerability assessment of buildings and infrastructure case studies
Coastal flooding and storm surge assessment case studies
Fluvial and pluvial flooding case studies
Space‑based inputs for disaster mitigation and management
Field works related to disaster management

reference_books: Singhal J.P, Disaster Management, Laxmi Publications.
Tushar Bhattacharya, Disaster Science and Management, McGraw Hill India.
Govt. of India, Disaster Management, Government of India.
